expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly restates the focus and breaks it into the three required areas under “Context-intake coverage expected for this benchmark focus”: “### 1) Component-stack constraints…”, “### 2) Embedding lifecycle assumptions”, and “### 3) Integration-sensitive customer expectations” including the customer reference “CVS Pharmacy, Inc. – CS0928640”. It also frames this as Phase 1 context intake requirements (spawn planning to capture inputs).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled “Phase 1 contract check” and states “Primary phase under test: phase1”. It describes Phase 1 as “spawn-only orchestration” and references the Phase 1 required output “phase1_spawn_manifest.json” and the “phase1.sh --post” validation step, repeatedly assessing compliance specifically against Phase 1 deliverables.
